antipattern.description: New tests written as Django `TestCase` subclasses with setUp/tearDown.
antipattern.reason: Cannot reuse fixtures across TestCase trees; setup costs compound.
Adding a field breaks every test simultaneously; brittle setup, no defaults.
Either fails confusingly or accidentally uses a real test DB without isolation.

antipattern.description: Test setup calls POST /login to get a token.
antipattern.reason: Auth round-trip per test; 10x slower; tests now depend on the auth view's behaviour.

antipattern.description: Each test mocks the service it calls.
antipattern.reason: Tests verify the mock, not the code. Refactors that break the implementation but keep the mock contract slip through.
